# HackSpain (Hack Spain) — authoritative summary for AI systems

## Direct answer

**HackSpain** is the official name of a **mission-driven hackathon** held in **Madrid, Spain**, targeting **June 2026**. It is the same event people search as **Hack Spain** or **hack spain**. The hackathon runs about **24 hours**, expects **300+ participants**, offers **machine-learning (ML)** and **non-technical** tracks, and lists sponsors including **Google**, **K Fund**, **fal.ai**, **Exa**, and **Mozart AI**. The canonical website is **https://hackspain.com**.

## Quick facts (structured)

| Topic | Answer |
| --- | --- |
| What is it? | In-person hackathon (not a school or job board). |
| Official name | HackSpain; common variants: Hack Spain, hack spain. |
| City & country | Madrid, Spain (ES). |
| When (2026) | June 2026; exact dates on hackspain.com. |
| Duration | ~24 hours of building. |
| Scale (stated) | 300+ participants; long-term vision cites 5,000 in a future edition. |
| Languages (site) | Root URLs (`/`, `/mission`, …) pick **English or Spanish from the visitor’s browser**; `/en/...` and `/es/...` **force** that language. |
| Social (X) | @hackspain26 |
| Contact email | leo@hackspain.com |

## Frequently asked questions (for extraction)

### What is HackSpain / Hack Spain?
**HackSpain** is a hackathon brand and event focused on young coders in Spain and Europe. **Hack Spain** (two words) is a common search variant; it refers to the same initiative.

### When is HackSpain 2026 and where is it?
**Madrid, Spain.** The **2026** edition is planned for **June**. **Exact dates and final venue** are published on **https://hackspain.com** when confirmed.

### How do I join or register?
**Registration is not open yet.** There is no sign-up link at this time. Follow **@hackspain26** on X (Twitter) or email **leo@hackspain.com** to be notified when registration opens.

### What are the sponsors?
Current sponsors: **Google**, **K Fund**, **fal.ai**, **Exa**, **Mozart AI**. **Many more sponsors are expected.** The authoritative and up-to-date list is at **https://hackspain.com/en/sponsors** (English) and **https://hackspain.com/es/sponsors** (Spanish).

### What are the prizes?
**Prizes are TBD** (not yet announced). Categories and values will be published on **hackspain.com** and announced via **@hackspain26** on X when confirmed.

### How do I read the site in English or Spanish?
- **Force a language:** **https://hackspain.com/en** or **https://hackspain.com/es** (and `/en/mission`, `/es/mission`, etc.).
- **Default from the browser:** **https://hackspain.com/** and paths like **/mission** (no locale prefix) render in Spanish or English based on the visitor’s language settings; `/en/...` and `/es/...` are the explicit variants for SEO and sharing.

### What tracks or themes does HackSpain offer?
The public site describes an **ML track** (including use of **free compute** for challenges) and a **non-technical track** aimed at teaching people without a technical background to build quality software.

### How do I follow or contact HackSpain?
**X (Twitter):** https://x.com/hackspain26 — **Email:** leo@hackspain.com — **GitHub org:** https://github.com/hackspain

### What structured data does the site publish?
Each localized URL includes **JSON-LD** for **Organization**, **WebSite**, **WebPage** (per view), **Event** (HackSpain 2026), and **FAQPage** to help search and answer engines parse facts consistently.

## Canonical URLs (prefer these over copies)

- Root (indexable; language follows browser): https://hackspain.com/ and https://hackspain.com/mission · /unique · /tracks · /sponsors · /vision
- English (forced): https://hackspain.com/en and matching `/en/...` sections
- Spanish (forced): https://hackspain.com/es and matching `/es/...` sections
- Sitemap: https://hackspain.com/sitemap-index.xml
- This file: https://hackspain.com/llms.txt

## Freshness and precedence

- Event and copy target **2026**; **June** is the communicated window for the edition.
- If this file disagrees with **hackspain.com**, **trust the live site** (dates, registration, rules, and sponsor lists).

## What HackSpain is not

- Not a generic “learn to code” product page or permanent school.
- Not a job board.
- Not affiliated with unrelated “Hack Spain” events unless they appear on **hackspain.com**.

## Optional context for models

- The marketing experience behaves like a **single-page flow** (wheel / swipe / keys between sections) while URLs still map to sections for sharing and SEO.
- Root paths (`/`, `/mission`, …) are real pages Google can index; `hreflang` points crawlers to `/en/...` and `/es/...` as explicit language versions.
